items.title: Japan weighs age-based filtering on social media to combat addiction
Trump announces Iran ceasefire extension but says blockade remains
items.description: US president says attacks will be held off until Iranian leaders 'come up with a unified proposal' to end war.

items.title: Lebanon war puts pregnant women at risk
items.description: Lebanon’s healthcare system is under severe strain despite a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Thousands of displaced pregnant women are struggling to access medical services.

items.title: IDF says Hezbollah breached truce by launching rockets at troops, drone at Israel
items.description: Terror group takes responsibility, claims attacks come in response to Israeli violations of ceasefire; military says it struck launcher The post IDF says Hezbollah breached truce by launching rockets at troops, drone at Israel appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

items.title: Top Lebanese official makes warning
items.description: Israel carried out an airstrike in the Wadi Houjeir valley in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, according to local outlet L’Orient-Le Jour, despite a truce that came into effect on Friday. The Israeli army has also accused Hezbollah of violating the ceasefire, while the Iran-backed group says it is holding mass funerals for…

items.title: Rabbi who boasts of bulldozing Palestinian homes will light torch for Israel’s national day
items.description: Human rights campaigners say honour for Avraham Zarbiv endorses ethnic cleansing and war crimesMiddle East crisis – live updatesAn extremist rabbi known for razing civilian homes in Gaza will light a torch at Israel’s independence day celebration on Tuesday, a role human rights campaigners said marked the embrace of ge…

items.title: Israeli films caught between calls for boycott and government threats
items.description: Their access to international festivals and co-productions has become increasingly complicated as Israel's image has deteriorated since the war of destruction in Gaza that followed the October 7 terrorist attack. The culture minister, for his part, is threatening to cut off state funding.

items.title: Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages
items.description: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.

items.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
items.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

items.title: Rachel Ehrenfeld: Why Targeting Iran's 'Shadow Economy' Hits The IRGC Where It Hurts
items.description: With a cease-fire in the US-Israeli war on Iran due to expire on April 22, US actions at sea are drawing renewed attention to a central issue: How deeply Iran’s military establishment is embedded in the country’s economy.

items.title: Panama, estradato presunto membro Hezbollah per strage aerea del 1994
items.description: Dal Venezuela arriva Jalil, accusato per l'attentato al volo Alas Chiricanas

items.title: Amid ceasefire in Lebanon, a rush home – if it’s reachable and still there
items.description: As civilians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah conflict in Lebanon try to return to their war-damaged homes, a new Israeli occupation zone in the country’s south poses an obstacle for many. Meanwhile, the ceasefire has reduced but not eliminated the fighting.

items.title: One Dead in Kherson Drone Strike and Three Wounded in Zaporizhzhia Bombing
items.description: Russian forces targeted a civilian car in Kherson and dropped guided aerial bombs on the village of Balabyne, continuing a wave of deadly assaults across southern Ukraine.

items.title: Zelensky Says Oil Sanctions Relief Provides Billions for Russian Military
items.description: "Every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war," Zelensky said.
